nodes.title: Guard the lower bound first
nodes.why: This exits early when the value is too small, so we do the minimum work and keep the logic easy to scan.
nodes.alternative: Use a nested `if/else` chain or a single expression with `Math.max`/`Math.min`.
nodes.tradeoff: Early returns are very readable, but they take a couple more lines than a compact one-liner.

nodes.title: Handle the upper bound as a second guard
nodes.why: After ruling out the lower-bound case, we only need one more check to cap oversized values.
nodes.alternative: Swap the check order and test `value > upper` first.
nodes.tradeoff: Either order works; keeping lower then upper is just a consistent left-to-right mental model.

nodes.title: Return unchanged value when already in range
nodes.why: If neither bound check triggered, the value is already valid and should pass through unchanged.
nodes.alternative: Compute the final value unconditionally with a math expression.
nodes.tradeoff: This explicit return is clear for learners, though it is slightly more verbose than expression style.

nodes.title: Raise value to the lower bound first
nodes.why: `Math.max` guarantees the intermediate result is never below `lower`.
nodes.alternative: Perform an explicit `if (value < lower)` guard instead.
nodes.tradeoff: Expression style is concise, but some readers need a second pass to decode nested calls.

nodes.title: Cap the intermediate value at the upper bound
nodes.why: Wrapping with `Math.min` enforces the upper limit after the lower-limit step.
nodes.alternative: Use two explicit branches for upper and lower checks.
nodes.tradeoff: Very compact and idiomatic, but debugging each step is less obvious than branch-by-branch code.

nodes.title: Keep clamp logic as one return expression
nodes.why: The entire rule reads as 'at least lower, at most upper' in one place.
nodes.alternative: Break it into intermediate variables for readability, like `const floored = ...`.
nodes.tradeoff: One line keeps the function short, while intermediate variables can be friendlier for newcomers.
